campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and where money was spent that month. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to make it easier for residents to get around using different methods like buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Multimodal transportation improvements (buses, bikes, pedestrian access)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice opinions on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward with introducing the ordinance and skipped the standard first reading process to speed things up. The ordinance is now scheduled to be voted on and officially adopted at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community feedback
New ordinance introduced and expedited (first reading waived)
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next meeting
Residents had opportunity to comment before council decision

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six previous meetings (March 16, 17, and 30)
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contract terms

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 7, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a routine meeting where they approved previous meeting notes and discussed funding and contracts for local improvements. Key items included securing a state grant to support the Mexican Heritage Plaza's arts and culture school, moving forward with traffic signal improvements on Bascom Avenue, and awarding a contract to repair streets and pavements across the city.
keyTopics: State grant awarded to Mexican Heritage Plaza for arts and culture education
Traffic signal project on Bascom Avenue moving to next phase
Street pavement repair and replacement project underway across local streets
City Council meeting minutes and committee reports approved

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and online participation options. Residents could join via Zoom or phone to watch the proceedings and speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone
Public comment procedures (raise hand feature or dial *9)
Unmuting instructions for phone participants (*6)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and designated special recognition for Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The meeting focused on celebrating these two important community observances during the scheduled special order of the day.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition and celebration
Fair Housing Month awareness and initiatives
Regular council meeting proceedings
Community heritage and civil rights observances

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss updates on the city's Active Transportation Plan, which focuses on making it easier and safer for people to walk, bike, and use other non-car transportation. The council also recognized April as both Donate Life Month and Fair Housing Month with proclamations. The meeting included time for residents to speak about any issues they wanted to bring before the council.
keyTopics: Active Transportation Plan Phase 2 progress and changes
Updated scoring system for evaluating transportation projects
Donate Life Month recognition
Fair Housing Month recognition
Public comment period for resident concerns

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and remote options so residents could participate however they preferred. The council provided multiple ways for people to join online via Zoom or phone, and allowed residents to submit written comments before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or join remotely via Zoom
Public comment submission available through eComments system
Zoom access details provided (Meeting ID and phone dial-in option)
Continued remote participation options for residents

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative matters, including updates to the city's federal funding plan (Consolidated Plan) for 2025-2030 and requests from individual council members for new projects or initiatives. The council also discussed changing the rules around how council vacancies are filled and reviewed upcoming meeting schedules.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 5-year federal funding and housing plan
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to the process for filling empty council seats
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility plans and rates for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved approving changes to how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service, with different rate adjustments for residential customers, apartment buildings, and large commercial users.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates for 2027 - changes to what residents pay for natural gas
Budget and financial forecast for the gas utility department
Reserve fund transfers and general fund contributions
Rate adjustments for three different customer categories (residential, master-metered/commercial, large commercial)
